name: Modern Potato Bread
recipeInstructions:
Makes 1 loaf
1. Combine 1/4 cup of the water, sugar, and yeast in a bowl. Stir to dissolve yeast and let stand until bubbly, about 5 minutes.
2. Fit the processor with a steel blade. In a work bowl, mix together flour, dry milk, potato flakes, 2 tablespoons of butter or margarine, and salt for about 15 seconds.
3. Add the yeast mixture to the flour mixture. Process until blended for about 10 seconds.
4. Turn on the processor and slowly drizzle in eggs and just enough remaining water through the feed tube into the flour mixture so that a dough forms a ball that cleans the sides of the bowl. Process until the ball rotates around the bowl about 25 times.
5. Turn off the processor and let the dough stand for 1 to 2 minutes.
6. Turn on the processor and gradually drizzle in enough remaining water to make the dough soft, smooth, and satin but not sticky. Process until the dough rotates around the bowl about 15 times.
7. Turn the dough onto a lightly greased surface. Shape it into a ball and place it on a greased cookie sheet. Roll or pat the dough into a circle about 8 inches in diameter. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let stand in a warm place until doubled, about 1 to 1 1/4 hours.
8. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). Uncover the bread and bake until golden and hollow when tapped, about 30 minutes.
9. Remove from the cookie sheet and cool on a wire rack.
